summary:
This file acts as a filter for the project's central code archive. It defines which files and folders should be kept private to individual developers' computers and excluded from the shared team repository.

**Business Behavior**
- Prevents sensitive information (like passwords, API keys, and local configuration) from being stored in the shared code archive.
- Keeps the central repository lean and fast by excluding automatically generated files, temporary data, and third-party libraries that can be recreated locally.
- Ensures all team members start with the same baseline configuration by tracking only template/example files, not local overrides.

**User Impact**
- Developers experience faster downloads, quicker code reviews, and smoother collaboration because the shared archive contains only essential source code.
- New team members can set up their work environment quickly using the provided example files, without manually searching through clutter.
- Reduces risk of accidental data leaks or broken builds caused by mismatched local settings or outdated temporary files.

**Rules**
- Automatically excludes: log files, testing reports, build outputs, cache folders, dependency libraries, local database files, and environment configuration files.
- Explicitly includes: example configuration files (`.env.example`, `.diffdocrc.example`) so they remain visible in the archive as reference templates.
- Applies to all standard development tools, package managers, and modern web frameworks used in the project.

**Data Movement**
- Blocks temporary, generated, and sensitive files from being uploaded to the central code storage.
- Allows only source code, documentation, and reference templates to flow into the shared repository.
- Keeps local development artifacts, caches, and personal settings isolated on individual machines.

**Visible Outcomes**
- The shared project archive remains clean, secure, and focused on actual application code.
- Team members never accidentally commit passwords, local paths, or massive generated folders.
- Onboarding and code synchronization are faster, more reliable, and require less manual cleanup.
raw_code_snapshot:
# Logs
logs
*.log
npm-debug.log*
yarn-debug.log*
yarn-error.log*
lerna-debug.log*

# Diagnostic reports (https://nodejs.org/api/report.html)
report.[0-9]*.[0-9]*.[0-9]*.[0-9]*.json

# Runtime data
pids
*.pid
*.seed
*.pid.lock

# Directory for instrumented libs generated by jscoverage/JSCover
lib-cov

# Coverage directory used by tools like istanbul
coverage
*.lcov

# nyc test coverage
.nyc_output

# Grunt intermediate storage (https://gruntjs.com/creating-plugins#storing-task-files)
.grunt

# Bower dependency directory (https://bower.io/)
bower_components

# node-waf configuration
.lock-wscript

# Compiled binary addons (https://nodejs.org/api/addons.html)
build/Release

# Dependency directories
node_modules/
jspm_packages/

# Snowpack dependency directory (https://snowpack.dev/)
web_modules/

# TypeScript cache
*.tsbuildinfo

# Optional npm cache directory
.npm

# Optional eslint cache
.eslintcache

# Optional stylelint cache
.stylelintcache

# Optional REPL history
.node_repl_history

# Output of 'npm pack'
*.tgz

# Yarn Integrity file
.yarn-integrity

# dotenv environment variable files
.env
.env.*
!.env.example
.diffdocrc
!.diffdocrc.example

# parcel-bundler cache (https://parceljs.org/)
.cache
.parcel-cache

# Next.js build output
.next
out

# Nuxt.js build / generate output
.nuxt
dist
.output

# Gatsby files
.cache/
# Comment in the public line in if your project uses Gatsby and not Next.js
# https://nextjs.org/blog/next-9-1#public-directory-support
# public

# vuepress build output
.vuepress/dist

# vuepress v2.x temp directory
.temp

# Sveltekit cache directory
.svelte-kit/

# vitepress build output
**/.vitepress/dist

# vitepress cache directory
**/.vitepress/cache

# Docusaurus cache and generated files
.docusaurus

# Serverless directories
.serverless/

# FuseBox cache
.fusebox/

# DynamoDB Local files
.dynamodb/

# Firebase cache directory
.firebase/

# TernJS port file
.tern-port

# Stores VSCode versions used for testing VSCode extensions
.vscode-test

# pnpm
.pnpm-store

# yarn v3
.pnp.*
.yarn/*
!.yarn/patches
!.yarn/plugins
!.yarn/releases
!.yarn/sdks
!.yarn/versions

# Vite files
vite.config.js.timestamp-*
vite.config.ts.timestamp-*
.vite/
.diffdoc/vectra/
